parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rule set contains 3 top-level rules (Natural Tendencies, Imparted Motion, Hindrance of Surroundings), well within the parsimony threshold. The three rules describe distinct phenomena and do not paraphrase each other. Each rule traces to specific observations (Rule 1 to obs 3,4,5,8,10,12; Rule 2 to obs 2,6,7; Rule 3 to obs 2,9,11), and the medium-resistance and natural-place language are permitted Aristotelian elaborations. With fewer than 5 rules, N12 hierarchy is not required, though the rules do cross-reference each other (e.g., the breeze contradiction between Rule 2 and Rule 3).
